name: Castro de As Muradellas Trail
name_original: Castro de As Muradellas — ida y vuelta desde el Santuario de la Tuiza
description: This easy 2.8-mile hiking route to Castro de As Muradellas from Santuario de la Tuiza offers ancient history and scenic views in just over a
region_intro: Hiking around Lubián offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Alta Sanabria region and the Sierra de la Culebra. The terrain is characterized by undulating mountains reaching around 1,000 meters, alongside pine, oak, and chestnut forests. Hikers will find a mix of valleys, streams, and open fields, providing varied scenery. This area is known for its rich flora and fauna, with unspoiled natural environments. Best hiking trails around Lubián The most popular hiking route is Bosque de Tejedelo — circular by Requejo de Sanabria and natural park Lago de Sanabria y Alrededores, a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 37 minutes to complete, winding through a natural park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cascadas de Aguas Cernidas — round trip from Terroso through the natural park Lago de Sanabria and sierras Segundera and de Porto, a moderate 7.5 miles (12.1 km) path, offering views of waterfalls and mountain ranges. Local hikers also love the Ascent to the Valdesirgas reservoir — circular from Porto through the Valdeinfierno meadows, a 8.8 miles (14.2 km) trail leading through meadows and towards a reservoir, often completed in about 4 hours 19 minutes. Hiking around Lubián is defined by undulating mountains, dense pine and oak forests, and tranquil valleys and streams. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents. The routes in Lubián are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Lubián's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Lubián?
A: Lubián offers a diverse range of hiking trails, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents. The area features undulating mountains, dense pine, oak, and chestnut forests, tranquil valleys, and streams. You'll find a mix of 2 easy routes, 5 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route, catering to various experience levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options near Lubián?
A: Yes, Lubián has several trails suitable for families. For an easy and scenic walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022985701/bosque-de-tejedelo-circular-by-requejo-de-sanabria-and-natural-park-lago-de-sanabria-y-alrededores" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque de Tejedelo — circular by Requejo de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.0 miles (4.8 km) long and winds through a natural park. Another gentle option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022978452/castro-de-as-muradellas-round-trip-from-the-santuario-de-la-tuiza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de As Muradellas - round trip from the Santuario de la Tuiza&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.8 miles (4.5 km) and starts from a cultural landmark.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Lubián?
A: Many trails in the Lubián area are dog-friendly, allowing you to explore the natural beauty with your canine companion. The region's unspoiled landscapes and varied terrain, including forests and open fields, provide ample space for dogs to enjoy. Always ensure your dog is under control and check local regulations for specific trails.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking around Lubián?
A: The region boasts several natural and cultural points of interest. You can discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4708534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of La Tuiza&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy scenic views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7989709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Puente Porto reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also known for the Sierra de la Culebra, a significant wolf stronghold with rich wildlife and diverse forests.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Lubián area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022985701/bosque-de-tejedelo-circular-by-requejo-de-sanabria-and-natural-park-lago-de-sanabria-y-alrededores" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque de Tejedelo — circular by Requejo de Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023150668/chestnut-path-and-meadows-of-hermisende-circular-through-the-valley" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chestnut path and meadows of Hermisende — circular through the Valley&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Lubián?
A: The best time for hiking in Lubián is generally during spring, early summer, and autumn. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, with blooming flora in spring and colorful foliage in autumn. The region's diverse scenery, from forests to open fields, is particularly enjoyable during these periods.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: Lubián is situated near the Sierra de la Culebra, an area known for its rich and healthy ecosystem. Hikers might spot deer, roe deer, and wild boar. The region is also a significant wolf stronghold in Spain, indicating a thriving natural environment. Bird-watching opportunities are abundant due to the diverse flora and tranquil streams.

Q: Is there parking available for hikers in Lubián?
A: Yes, Lubián and its surrounding villages typically offer parking options for hikers. Many trailheads or nearby village centers provide designated parking areas. It's advisable to check specific starting points for the most convenient parking, especially for popular routes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Lubián?
A: The hiking trails around Lubián are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the serene surroundings, the diverse landscapes of forests and open fields, and the opportunities to immerse oneself in unspoiled nature.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, Lubián has a rich cultural history. The village is a key point on the Camino Sanabrés, a branch of the Camino de Santiago, offering a blend of spiritual and scenic walks. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431365" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of La Tuiza&lt;/a&gt;, a site of cultural interest, or explore the unique **Cortello dei Lobi**, a circular stone enclosure used by ancient hunters to trap wolves.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, you can experience waterfalls on some trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023013364/cascadas-de-aguas-cernidas-round-trip-from-terroso-through-the-natural-park-lago-de-sanabria-and-sierras-segundera-and-de-porto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascadas de Aguas Cernidas — round trip from Terroso&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.5 miles (12.1 km) path that offers views of waterfalls and mountain ranges within the natural park Lago de Sanabria and sierras Segundera and de Porto.
tips:
Beautiful sanctuary in the heart of Sanabria Alta. Close to the highway, it is worth taking a detour to visit this spectacular large hermitage, which surely will not leave us indifferent.

Nearby, we will find a river with a fluvial beach area, and a hiking route to the Castro de As Muradellas. Or also the Tío mill.

The construction dates from the 18th century and two important pilgrimages are held (August 5 in honor of the Virgen de las Nieves and the last Sunday of September in honor of La Tuiza).

Picturesque bridge that crosses the Tuela river near the Tuiza Sanctuary and that brings us closer to the Tío Juan maquiler mill along a beautiful path between oaks and chestnut trees up to the
Castro de As Muradellas next to the river. The area is perfectly signposted with informative and indicative signs.

At this point we find ourselves in a meander on which the castro of "As Muradellas" sits, an Astur settlement from the 3rd century BC. C. (the Iron Age) where remains of defensive structures such as walls and moats are preserved. It also preserves remains of what was the town. In this enclave we can also enjoy magnificent views of the gorges and meanders formed by the Tuela river.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail for the Castro de As Muradellas round trip begins at the Santuario de la Tuiza. While specific parking details aren't provided, it's common for sanctuaries of this nature to have nearby parking facilities for visitors. The sanctuary is a significant landmark in the Sanabria Alta region, making it a clear starting point.

faqs.question: Is this hike suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this hike is classified as an easy hike and is considered suitable for any fitness level. The paths are easily accessible, making it a great option for beginners and families looking for an enriching outdoor experience.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this round trip?
faqs.answer: The round trip from the Santuario de la Tuiza to Castro de As Muradellas is approximately 4.52 km long and typically takes about 1 hour and 16 minutes to complete.

faqs.question: What historical sites or natural features can I see along the trail?
faqs.answer: This route offers a blend of history and natural beauty. You'll start at the 18th-century Sanctuary of La Tuiza, a significant cultural landmark. The main highlight is the Castro de As Muradellas, a fascinating Celtic archaeological site from the 3rd century BC, known for its defensive structures and views over the Tuela River gorge. You'll also cross the Río Tuela Bridge, and enjoy the natural surroundings of the Sanabria Alta region.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail takes hikers through natural surroundings, including an uphill section through forests. The paths are easily accessible, leading to the Castro de As Muradellas which is strategically located on a promontory. Expect varied natural scenery, including views of the Tuela River.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the specific rules for dogs on this exact trail are not detailed, many natural areas in Spain are dog-friendly, especially on leash. Given its easy classification and natural setting, it's likely suitable for dogs on a leash. Always ensure your dog is under control and respect local signage regarding pets.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Sanabria Alta region, where this trail is located, is known for its natural beauty. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, making them ideal for hiking. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially if you start early to avoid the midday heat. Winter hiking is possible, but check weather conditions as the area is part of a high mountain range.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to hike the Castro de As Muradellas round trip or to visit the Santuario de la Tuiza or the Castro itself. Access to these natural and historical sites is generally free.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is explicitly described as a "round trip from the Santuario de la Tuiza," indicating it is a loop trail that brings you back to your starting point.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 18% of this route follows sections of the Camino Sanabrés (East) and the Mtb Camino Sanabrés (Este) / Mtb Camiño Xabrés (Leste).

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to eat near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The area around the Santuario de la Tuiza is described as beautiful, nestled in the heart of Sanabria Alta. While specific cafes or restaurants at the sanctuary itself are not mentioned, the nearby towns of Lubián and Hermisende would likely offer amenities such as cafes, pubs, and accommodation for visitors to the region.
